ground_truth.title: Love Good Strong Coffee? Check Out These Top Espresso Makers
ground_truth.author: Charlotte Pointing
ground_truth.main_content:
Most of us can't get through the day without a caffeine hit. According to research from Statista, more than 73 percent of Americans drink coffee every single day. While you can't go wrong with a latte, sometimes only something stronger will do: espresso. You don't need to leave the house to get this caffeine kick—there are plenty of efficient espresso makers on the market, from luxury to budget-friendly options.

Espresso was invented in Italy in the early 20th century and is the ultimate strong black coffee, made by forcing high-pressured hot water through finely ground coffee beans. It forms the basis of lattes and cappuccinos, which work just as well with dairy-free milk. Adding hot water creates an Americano, invented by American soldiers in World War II.

The best espresso makers from budget to luxury:

1. Bialetti Moka Express, $54.95 - Italian-made stovetop option, affordable, durable, easy to use, and portable.

2. Wacaco Nanopresso, $51.92 - Portable handheld option, lightweight yet powerful, capable of reaching 18 bars of stable pressure.

3. L'OR Barista System Coffee and Espresso Machine Combo, $159 - Compact dual coffee and espresso machine from L'OR by Philips, great results for under $200.

4. De'Longhi Stilosa Manual, $99.95 - Easy-to-use, sleek espresso machine with ergonomic portafilter, adjustable cup holder, stainless steel boiler, and manual milk frother.

5. Capresso Café TS Touchscreen, $175.99 - Touchscreen espresso machine with warming tray, 15-bar pressure, frothing wand, and hot water button.

6. Breville Infuser, $565.97 - Features a built-in pressure gauge to help achieve rich full flavor, balanced taste, velvety mouthfeel, and irresistible body.

7. Rancilio Silvia Espresso Machine, $995 - Traditional espresso machine with sturdy iron frame, stainless steel exterior, and commercial-grade group head for top-quality caffeine.
with: More than 73 percent of Americans drink coffee every single day.
Espresso is made by forcing a small amount of high-pressured hot water through finely ground coffee beans to produce a concentrated shot of coffee.
Bialetti Moka Express is an Italian-made stovetop option that is affordable, durable, easy to use, and timeless.
The Breville Infuser features a built-in pressure gauge specifically designed to help create a perfect espresso with rich, full flavor.
Rancilio Silvia has a sturdy iron frame, stainless steel exterior, and a commercial-grade group head for top-quality caffeine.

items.alt: Love Good Strong Coffee? Check Out These Top Espresso Makers
